reference: Hi. This occurs due to age-related hardening of blood vessels called arteriosclerosis. This hardening is not reversible, which is why he has high blood pressure. He should be taking medication throughout his life. He should maintain a healthy lifestyle, such as avoiding fatty, oily, and high-calorie foods. He should follow a low-sodium diet and monitor his blood pressure regularly, three times a day for one week, and then once or twice a week thereafter. If the blood pressure is persistently more than one hundred and forty over ninety millimeters of mercury, then his medications will need to be increased. Regular exercise, like brisk walking, can help him. He can walk according to his capacity for at least thirty minutes a day, five days a week.
